title: IRCA — Immigration Reform and Control Act
description: Reagan signs IRCA granting amnesty to ~3 million undocumented immigrants and imposing employer sanctions
notes: First major amnesty — major theme in Tigres del Norte corridos

title: Mexican Presidential Election 1988
description: PRI's Carlos Salinas de Gortari declared winner amid widespread fraud claims; Cuauhtémoc Cárdenas claims victory
notes: Formative political crisis; 'fraude electoral' trope enters popular culture

title: Arrest of Miguel Ángel Félix Gallardo
description: 'El Padrino' — founder of Guadalajara Cartel arrested; leads to fragmentation into Sinaloa/Tijuana/Juárez cartels
notes: Watershed moment in cartel history; directly referenced in corridos

title: George H.W. Bush inaugurated as 41st President
description: 41st US President inaugurated; continues Reagan-era drug war and immigration policies

title: George H.W. Bush — Drug War escalation
description: Bush administration increases DEA budget and border enforcement; signs 1990 Crime Control Act

title: Bill Clinton inaugurated as 42nd President
description: 42nd US President inaugurated; NAFTA ratification and expanded border enforcement follow

title: NAFTA approved by US Congress
description: House passes NAFTA; takes effect Jan 1 1994. Displaces Mexican agricultural workers → surge in migration
notes: Economic shock cited as migration driver in literature and corridos

title: NAFTA enters into force
description: North American Free Trade Agreement effective; eliminates tariffs; devastates Mexican smallholder agriculture

title: EZLN Zapatista uprising in Chiapas
description: Indigenous Zapatista Army rises against NAFTA and neoliberalism; 12 days of fighting; ongoing political insurgency
notes: Coincides with NAFTA — twin symbolic events of 1994

title: Mexican Presidential Election 1994
description: Ernesto Zedillo (PRI) elected; Colosio previously assassinated. PRI dominance continues despite pressure

title: Operation Gatekeeper launched in San Diego
description: INS launches massive border enforcement in San Diego sector — pushes crossings into deadly Sonoran desert
notes: Directly increases migrant deaths; major corrido theme

title: California Proposition 187 passes
description: Voters approve measure barring undocumented immigrants from public services; later ruled unconstitutional
notes: Galvanized Latino political identity; echoed in Tigres lyrics

title: Amado Carrillo Fuentes at peak of power
description: 'El Señor de los Cielos' controls Juárez Cartel at height of power, largest cocaine transporter in Mexico
notes: Subject of famous narcocorrido; Los Tucanes connection

title: Hector Palma Salazar arrested
description: 'El Güero Palma' of Sinaloa Cartel arrested; Chapo Guzmán becomes dominant

title: Death of Amado Carrillo Fuentes
description: El Señor de los Cielos dies during plastic surgery; significant shift in Juárez Cartel power
notes: Multiple corridos written about his death

title: Mexican Presidential Election 2000 — PAN wins
description: Vicente Fox (PAN) defeats PRI ending 71-year single-party rule; historic democratic transition
notes: Massive cultural moment — end of PRI era reflected in lyrics

title: George W. Bush inaugurated as 43rd President
description: 43rd US President inaugurated; post-9/11 border securitization dramatically reshapes immigration enforcement

title: First capture of Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán
description: El Chapo arrested at Puente Grande prison; escapes in 2001 in laundry cart

title: El Chapo escapes from Puente Grande prison
description: Guzmán escapes maximum security prison; becomes most wanted fugitive in Mexico
notes: Fodder for numerous narcocorridos

title: Secure Fence Act — signed December 2005
description: Congress authorizes 700 miles of border fence; construction accelerates 2006-2009

title: Great American Boycott — immigration marches
description: Millions march across US cities on May 1 against HR 4437 (criminalization of undocumented immigrants); largest immigrant mobilization in US history
notes: Los Tigres del Norte performed at rallies

title: Mexican Presidential Election 2006 — Calderón vs AMLO
description: Felipe Calderón (PAN) narrowly defeats Andrés Manuel López Obrador amid fraud controversy; AMLO refuses to concede
notes: Deeply polarizing; left vs right narrative in corridos

title: Calderón launches War on Drugs
description: Felipe Calderón deploys 6,500 troops to Michoacán; beginning of 6-year militarized drug war
notes: Turning point — narco violence explodes; defines entire era of corridos

title: Plan Mérida Initiative negotiations begin
description: US-Mexico security cooperation framework; $1.4 billion aid package for drug war

title: Mérida Initiative signed into law
description: Bush signs $400M first installment of Mérida Initiative security cooperation

title: Barack Obama inaugurated as 44th President
description: 44th US President inaugurated; later signs DACA (2012) and enacts record deportations 2009-2016

title: Peak cartel violence in Ciudad Juárez
description: Juárez becomes murder capital of world; 2,600+ homicides in 2009 alone; civilian massacre at Villas de Salvarcar

title: Villas de Salvarcar massacre
description: Gunmen kill 16 teenagers at a student party in Juárez; Calderón initially dismisses victims as gang members

title: Los Zetas formal split from Gulf Cartel
description: Los Zetas break from Gulf Cartel; massive territorial war begins across northeast Mexico
notes: Major narrative shift in narcocorridos — Zetas become primary villain figure

title: San Fernando massacre — 193 migrants killed
description: Zetas massacre 193 Central American migrants at San Fernando Tamaulipas; worst mass killing of migrants
notes: Intersection of migration and narco themes

title: DACA announced by Obama
description: Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals — protection for ~800,000 DREAMers; no deportation + work permits
notes: Major policy moment; generational shift in immigration debate

title: Mexican Presidential Election 2012 — PRI returns
description: Enrique Peña Nieto (PRI) wins; PRI returns to power after 12-year PAN interlude

title: Arrest of Mario Cárdenas Guillén (Gulf Cartel)
description: Senior Gulf Cartel figure arrested; continues fragmentation of Gulf organization

title: First re-arrest of El Chapo Guzmán
description: Captured at Mazatlán hotel; federal forces coordinated operation
notes: Major narcocorrido event

title: Ayotzinapa — 43 students disappeared
description: 43 normalista students from Ayotzinapa Rural Teachers College disappear in Iguala Guerrero; state complicity alleged
notes: Defining atrocity of Peña Nieto era; massive cultural/lyrical response

title: El Chapo second prison escape
description: Escapes through 1.5km tunnel from Altiplano prison; largest manhunt in Mexico

title: El Chapo final recapture
description: Captured in Los Mochis following Sean Penn Rolling Stone interview; later extradited to US

title: Donald Trump inaugurated as 45th President
description: Campaign centered on border wall and mass deportation; immediate immigration executive orders

title: Trump border wall executive order
description: Executive Order 13767 — directs construction of border wall, increased deportations, end of catch-and-release

title: Trump travel ban (Executive Order 13769)
description: Bans nationals from 7 Muslim-majority countries; signals restrictionist turn; massive protests

title: DACA rescinded by Trump administration
description: Sessions announces end of DACA program; ~700,000 recipients face deportation threat

title: Zero Tolerance policy announced
description: Sessions announces 100% criminal prosecution of illegal border crossers; leads to family separations

title: Trump ends family separation policy
description: Executive Order signed after global outcry; 2,700+ children already separated from parents

title: Mexican Presidential Election 2018 — AMLO wins
description: Andrés Manuel López Obrador (MORENA) wins with 53%; first left-wing president in modern era
notes: Historic shift; Cuarta Transformación rhetoric

title: Culiacanazo — El Chapo's son released
description: Mexican security forces capture Ovidio Guzmán but release him after Sinaloa cartel paralyzes Culiacán with gunmen; humiliation for AMLO government
notes: Major narco-political event; widely discussed

title: MPP / Remain in Mexico policy begins
description: Migrant Protection Protocols force asylum seekers to wait in Mexico; creates tent cities at border

title: El Chapo sentenced to life in US prison
description: Convicted on all 10 counts by Brooklyn federal jury; sentenced to life + 30 years

title: Biden elected — defeats Trump
description: Joe Biden wins 2020 presidential election; reverses many Trump immigration policies

title: Joe Biden inaugurated as 46th President
description: Reverses Trump travel bans; reinstates DACA; new asylum policies

title: CJNG consolidates dominance
description: Cartel Jalisco Nueva Generación under Nemesio Oseguera ('El Mencho') becomes most powerful cartel nationally

title: Sinaloa Cartel factions fracture — Chapitos vs Mayos
description: Internal Sinaloa conflict between sons of El Chapo (Chapitos) and faction of El Mayo (Zambada)

title: Claudia Sheinbaum elected President of Mexico
description: First woman president in Mexican history; MORENA continues; takes office Oct 1 2024

title: Donald Trump inaugurated as 47th President (2nd term)
description: Declares national emergency at border; designates cartels as terrorist organizations; mass deportation operations

title: Alien Enemies Act invoked for deportations
description: Trump invokes 1798 Alien Enemies Act to expedite deportation of alleged Tren de Aragua/cartel members

title: Camarena murder — DEA agent killed
description: DEA Agent Enrique Camarena kidnapped and murdered by Guadalajara Cartel; led to Operation Leyenda and US-Mexico drug war cooperation
notes: Pivotal event; accelerated cartel crackdowns

title: George H.W. Bush — Drug War escalation
description: Bush administration intensifies domestic war on drugs; increases DEA and border enforcement budgets

title: Operation Hold the Line — El Paso
description: Border Patrol strategy of 'prevention through deterrence' in El Paso sector deployed nationally

title: Gulf Cartel hires Zetas as armed wing
description: Former elite Mexican soldiers (GAFE) form Los Zetas as enforcement arm of Gulf Cartel under Osiel Cárdenas

title: Juárez Cartel vs Sinaloa war begins
description: Bloodiest cartel conflict in Mexican history begins in Ciudad Juárez; 10,000+ killed 2008-2012

title: Trump elected President
description: Anti-immigration platform central to campaign; galvanizes both deportation fears and resistance

title: Ronald Reagan inaugurated as 40th President
description: Begins anti-communist foreign policy; drug war rhetoric; economic policies affecting Mexican labor migration

title: 9/11 attacks — border security tightens
description: Terrorist attacks lead to massive border securitization; INS reorganized into DHS; immigration enforcement hardened

title: Mexican Peso Crisis (Tequila Crisis)
description: Peso devaluation triggers economic crisis; unemployment surges; migration to US increases dramatically
notes: Strong economic driver for migration themes

title: Great American Boycott — May Day marches
description: 500,000+ march in Los Angeles; Tigres del Norte performed; "Un Día sin Inmigrantes" movement
notes: Los Tigres del Norte politically engaged moment

title: Caballeros Templarios founded in Michoacán
description: Knights Templar Cartel emerges from ashes of La Familia Michoacana; quasi-religious narco structure
